observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory on all PPE accounts and submit an updated RPCPPE;

Require the GSO to maintain updated Property Cards by category to record the acquisition, disposal, and other information about the PPE and ensure that PCs are reconciled with the PPELCs maintained by the accounting unit and with the updated RPCPPE; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to record the equipment to its appropriate PPE account. Likewise ensure that the correct computation and recording of the depreciation in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the total value of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit Statement of Work Accomplished and Certificate of Completion to the Municipal Accountant upon project completion to serve as the basis for transferring the completed projects from the CIP to their appropriate PPE accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Inventory Committee together with the Municipal Engineer and GSO to conduct an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain LRNLC tor roads and road components for every road network; and

Direct the GSO to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) and submit to the COA Office the updated RPCPPE and RPCLRN.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


Direct the Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate conduct of disposal of all unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386; and

Request in writing to COA Office the disposal of the unserviceable properties supported by required documents.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the property custodian and Accountant to attach the repair and maintenance history/record to the disbursement vouchers for repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject property assets upon securing the Certificate of Title using the actual costs or appraised value if the former could not be determined; and

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the ₱1.450 million recorded under Construction in Progress-Land Improvements to accurately reflect the nature of the transactions and correct the overstatement of assets and government equity; and
Strictly adhere to the criteria outlined in IPSAS 17 when recognizing Property, Plant, and Equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit Certificate of Completion related to the mentioned infrastructure projects to the Municipal Accountant to serve as the basis for the reclassification of completed projects from the CIP to their appropriate PPE accounts;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cost of the completed projects immediately upon receipt of the Certificate of Completion from the Municipal Engineering Office; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the depreciation expenses of the PPE items starting from the date of completion.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately rectify the erroneous depreciation computations by excluding the residual values and draw necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation; and
Ensure that depreciation for road networks is computed in strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, specifically the requirement.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and recognize the depreciation expenses for all PPE items that were not subjected to depreciation to accurately reflect the correct depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation; and
Ensure compliance with Section 2.1 of COA Circular Letter No. 2004-003 dated October 4, 2004, which require depreciation to begin a month after an asset is completed or acquired.
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of Trust Liability – DRRM account in the Financial Statements;
Ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balances of the LDRRMF are fully transferred to the Trust Liability – DRRM account in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 regardless of any shortfall in actual revenue collections; and
Strengthen monitoring of LDRRMF utilization to ensure that unexpended balances in the STF are promptly reverted to the General Fund once the five-year validity period has lapsed.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Consider evaluating the current designation of the General Services Officer (GSO) to ensure that the personnel assigned possesses the appropriate qualifications, skills, and competencies required for effective inventory management, and provide necessary training or support interventions to enhance performance, if needed;
Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) – designate to strictly maintain and regularly update stock cards for all the inventory items; and
Require the immediate conduct of semestral physical count of inventories in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume I and ensure that the corresponding Reports on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) are duly prepared and submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish at the beginning of the year the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax receivables in accordance with Section 20 of the MNGAS;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to include both current and delinquent real property taxes in the certified list of collectibles to ensure the reliability of total amount due and collectible; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the updating and regular maintenance of the Real Property Tax Account Register and the Taxpayer’s Index Cards and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the start of each year as basis for drawing a Journal Entry Voucher for the setup of RPT and SET receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the Local Economic Enterprises and 20% Development Fund, in accordance with Section 313 of Republic Act No. 7160 and the applicable provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I;
Require the Municipal Accountant to use separate subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise and development fund project, as prescribed; and
Ensure the preparation of complete and separate financial statements for each special account, such as Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet), Statement of Financial Performance (Statement of Income and Expenses), and Statement of Cash Flows and attach these to the General Fund trial balance as part of the year-end financial reporting requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and concerned personnel to strictly adhere to the documentary requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001, particularly the submission of duly approved Daily Time Records (DTRs) and, where applicable, service records and special orders, to substantiate claims for Hazard Duty Pay and Subsistence Allowance.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to formulate and submit the required three-year GAD Agenda in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure strategic, well-aligned, and measurable approach to gender mainstreaming initiatives; and
Direct the GFPS to prepare the annual GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with the GAD Agenda to ensure that identified programs and projects will answer GAD issues that directly relates to the GAD goals of the Municipality for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Local Economic Development and Investment Promotions Officer (LEDIPO) to prepare the mandatory 5-year Business Plan for the Local Economic Enterprise in accordance with Section 5.3 of the Manual on the Setting up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises;
Integrate the LEE’s Business Plan into the Local Development Plan of the municipality, as this will ensure proper alignment with the LGU’s overall development goals and allow for appropriate budgeting and planning at the local level; and
Direct the personnel concerned to prepare and submit the Semi-annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review reports using the format prescribed in Annex B as these reports are necessary to monitor the financial performance of the LEE, evaluate progress toward full cost recovery, and make data-driven decisions on operational adjustments.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the unrecorded PPE accounts and ensure the correct computation and recording of the depreciation and accumulated depreciation in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2003-007 to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the total value of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to furnish the Property Custodian Designate and the Municipal Accountant, the documents necessary to record the receipt and distribution of vaccines, ancillaries and supplies Inventory Reports or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD; and draw journal entry voucher for the applicable inventory accounts to record the remaining vaccines, ancillaries and supplies in stock at December 31, 2021 and December 31, 2022.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to initiate massive tax campaign for efficient and aggressive tax collection

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and effect deductions of liquidated damages upon final payment; and

Henceforth, comply with the provisions of 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184 and Local Government Procurement manual on the imposition of liquidated damages.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to effectively implement programs, projects and activities under the 20 percent Development Fund;

Recommend to the Municipal Planning Development Officer and to include only PPAs that are feasible, and can be completed within the target date of implementation taking into consideration the priorities set by the LGU leadership; and

Closely supervise PPAs implementation and to monitor its execution so problems/issues that may arise shall be immediately addressed.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Hire MENRO or create a position to handle and take charge of the operations and maintenance of MENRO office.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the enforcement of fines and penalties to violators of prohibited acts as provided in the IRR of RA 9003.

Direct the Municipal Health Officer and/or concerned RHU personnel to furnish the Property Custodian Designate and the Municipal Accountant, the documents necessary to record the receipt and distribution of vaccines, ancillaries and supplies Inventory Reports or its equivalent in relation to the vaccination program; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record using the perpetual inventory system, the vaccines received and its distribution as reported by the LGU to DOH-CHD; and draw journal entry voucher for the applicable inventory accounts to record the remaining vaccines, ancillaries and supplies in stock at December 31, 2021 and December 31, 2022.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Ensure that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to establish and record RPT Receivable and SET Receivable, at the beginning of the year, based on the certified list of RPT Accounts Register/Tax Index for the current year provided by the Municipal Treasurer using the following entries:

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to initiate massive tax campaign for efficient and aggressive tax collection; 



Enforce the remedies provided under the following Section 258 and 259 RA 7160 to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT; and

Seek the assistance from the 26 barangays in the Municipality for the implementation of municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of notices of delinquency and serving warrants of levy to the delinquent real property owners with their territorial jurisdiction,

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to explain in writing discrepancies noted in the Statement of Work Accomplished Items Nos. 8, 10, and 11 prepared by the contractor which was not correctly validated/checked and did not reflect the actual work done;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and effect deductions of liquidated damages upon final payment; and



Henceforth, comply with the provisions of 2016 revised IRR of RA 9184 and Local Government Procurement manual on the imposition of liquidated damages.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Engineering office and LDRRM departments to fast-track implementation of capital program and mitigate flooding incidents; and

Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) and Local Disaster Risk Reduction Council to prepare an updated LDRRMF integrating the priority projects to be funded from the Special Trust Funds for pre-disaster prevention and mitigation activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Provide sufficient funding to ensure that the implementation of all projects, programs, and activities under Solid Waste Management Programs are carried out and in accordance with the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget; and

Provide budget for the continuation of perimeter fence at Material Recovery Facility to safeguard the premises.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Make a written representation to DENR-EMB on the replacement of defective Biodegradable Waste Shredder; and 
Hire MENRO or create a position to handle and take charge of the operations and maintenance of MENRO office.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to return unused balances of fund transfers to the Bureau of Treasury on the completed projects; and

Secure written approval from the source agency for an authority to continue the use of FALGU funds due to the late receipt of fund transfer for projects/programs that have not been completed/implemented; and

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to implement adequate measures to ensure the timely submission of the financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate auditorial review; and

 Henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws, rules, and regulations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to:

Reconstitute the Inventory committee and prepare Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) specifying the specific assignments, the cut-off date, specifying the schedule dates and location of inventory taking activities from start to the targeted date of completion as provided in section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the property custodian to reconcile and adjust the difference between books of Accounts and the property records respectively, as warranted; and 

Require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to look into and comply with the guidelines and procedures set forth in the COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address issues and concerns on the non-existing and unserviceable assets.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustment and draw journal entry voucher to record the receipt, disbursement and distribution of fund sources from cash and in-kind donations, if warranted 

Ensure that all future donations during the implementation of community quarantine or state of calamity, whether in cash or in-kind, are properly receipted/acknowledged, recorded, accounted for, utilized, distributed, and reported without necessarily disregarding basic controls and issuances governing donations or ads during a disaster or pandemic; and

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Property Custodian and the Accountant to maintain a repair and maintenance record/history of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the Municipality to track down the frequency of repair and to avoid unnecessary repair and maintenance expenses;

Direct the property custodian and Accountant to attach the repair and maintenance history/record to the disbursement vouchers for repairs and maintenance expenses as supporting documents.

Direct the Accountant and the Property Custodian to determine if major repairs can add up the life of the motor vehicles and recompute the depreciation expense to the remaining life of the asset.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the Municipal Accountant;

We recommended that Management:

Strictly implement all projects, programs and activities of its mandatory funds in relation to its Annual Budget;

Require the GAD Focal Person, LDRRM Officer and the PWD Focal Person that during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, include only those which are feasible and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership; and

See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so that problems/issues that may arise during the implementation shall be immediately addressed.

We recommended and the management agreed to:


   a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to justify why  official  receipts  are not issued to acknowledge cash donations from private individuals  and organizations and stop the practice of utilizing donation in cash that are not properly recorded; and


   b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary  adjustment  and draw journal entry voucher to record  the  receipt,  disbursement  and distribution of fund sources  from  cash  and  in-kind  donations,  if warranted;

Ensure  that  all  future  donations  during  the  implementation  of community quarantine or state of calamity, whether in cash or in kind, are  properly   receipted/acknowledged,   recorded,   accounted   for, utilized, distributed, and reported without  necessarily  disregarding basic controls and issuances governing  donations  or  aids  during  a disaster or pandemic; and


   d. Submit immediately the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF  for CY 2021 in required format without delay.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Consider feasibility of providing computer software/system to
facilitate the completion of database of real property units and for
more efficient and effective administration of the real property tax;
28
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the duly certified list showing
the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to the
Municipal Accountant;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning
of the year and make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct income for
the reporting period; and
d. Adopt the Accrual Method of accounting real property taxes in accordance with
Paragraph 1 of IPSAS and Section 246 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, wages, allowances, etc. by opening of
individual ATM savings accounts through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)
with any of the government servicing bank stationed in Roxas City;
b. Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA and make
representations for possible establishment of the applicable type of ATM to the
premises of the LGU; and
c. Limit access to unclaimed payrolls to the disbursing officer only.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue a Demand Letter to concern officers and
employees and personnel’s who are no longer in government service and/or
transferred to other government offices;
b. If after demand have been served and accountable officers still failed to settle
their outstanding balances, require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the
Office of the Auditor an updated list of accountable officers with outstanding cash
advances as of January 31,2020, including an aging of the cash advance in such
for showing the purpose of the cash advances, date granted and other
information required such as disbursement voucher number, Journal Entry
Voucher number on the grant of the cash advance, as basis for appropriate
administrative action; and
c. Enforce strict compliance to Section 4.1.2 of COA circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10,1997 requiring no additional cash advances shall be allowed to any
official or employee unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper
accounting is made thereof.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Facilitate the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to
establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU thereof
pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Direct the Municipal Assessor to appraise the untitled lots to its current market
value and provide the same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in recording
in the book of accounts;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the subject property assets upon
securing the Certificate of Title using the actual costs or appraised value if the
former could not be determined; and
d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper payments of
expenses for the land titling and to update and reconcile the inventory of property
assets with the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Provide sufficient funding for the construction of perimeter fence and installation
of water system in the Crisis Center to make it fully functional in order to provide
temporary shelter and adequate services to individuals and families in crisis
situation;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to expedite the development of a fully functional
Crisis Center as soon as the budget becomes available for use;
c. Instruct the MSWDO and GAD Focal Person to evaluate the efficiency and
effectiveness of activities implemented for prevention, intervention and
reintegration in addressing issues of violence, poverty and vulnerability to
unexpected crisis;
d. Align local budget plans, as far as practicable, with national development plans,
goals, and strategies in order to optimize the utilization of resources; and
e. Establish linkages with NGAs that advocates the protection of women’s and
children rights and sought their participation to appropriate GAD activities being
implemented.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Strictly implement all projects, programs and activities of its mandatory funds in
relation to its Annual Budget;
b. Require the GAD Focal Person, LDRRM Officer and the PWD Focal Person that
during identification of PPA’s for budgeting, include only those which are feasible
and can be completed within the target date of implementation, taking into
consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership; and
c. See to it that PPAs being implemented are closely monitored and supervised so
that problems/issues that may arise during the implementation shall be
immediately addressed.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to initiate massive tax campaign for efficient and aggressive tax collection;
Adopt measures and formulate strategies such as installation of Real Property Tax and Collection System to improve revenue collection efficiency;
Enforce the remedies provided under the following Sections of the Code in order to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT:
Section 256 – Application of remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action; and
Section 259 – Imposition of penalties for failure to issue and execute warrant of levy; and
Seek assistance from the 26 barangays in the Municipality for the implementation of municipality’s tax campaign program, particularly in the dissemination of notices of delinquency and serving the warrants of levy to the delinquent real property owners within their territorial jurisdiction.

We recommended that Management to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund allocated for the implementation of various PPAs identified in the LDRRMFIP as priorities particularly on its Prevention and Mitigation aspects to limit or avoid potential adverse impact of hazard and related disaster and as to not deprive the constituents of the benefits that would be derived from the implementation of the said PPAs.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management:
Direct the Designated Property Custodian to furnished Municipal Accountant the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as well as the corrected RPCPPE;
Reconcile the accounting records against the corrected RPCPPE as to the remaining existing properties of the LGU and reclassify those PPE that has already reached its salvage value to “Other Asset” account; and
Draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the unserviceable properties and apply appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the corresponding depreciation and make necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, instruct the latter to keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network as required in the Circular.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Consider the 20% debt service ceiling of the Municipality and its net available
funds prior to contracting of loans/borrowings pursuant to DOF LFC No. 1-
2012;
b. Consider the remaining term of the contracting incumbent official in
determining the loan/borrowing terms;
c. Have a proper evaluation for the alignment of loans to support development
plans and investment programs identifying their priority projects particularly
income generating projects;
d. Maintain updated subsidiary ledgers for each loan/borrowings contracted for
proper monitoring of the correct balances of the accounts and propriety of
expenses charged thereon; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to include adequate disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements regarding the loan payable and its related accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to initiate the
compilation of information for all items of PPE (current and prior years) at
cost or fair value, duly supported with Construction in Progress Ledger Card
(CIPLC);
b. Reclassify CIP account to appropriate asset account, as soon as the
construction is completed including all expenses during the construction
period;
c. Henceforth, observe accuracy and timely recording and maintenance of
CIPLC containing all on-going and uncompleted projects for proper
monitoring;
d. Prepare the specific estimated useful life for each asset based on LGU’s
experience on the life of its PPE, copy furnished the Audit Team; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure in the Notes
to Financial Statements regarding the status of any item in the PPE funded
by a loan.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendations that Management
direct the Heads of Property and Accounting Offices to reconcile the recorded
accountability of PPE compared with the existing PPE at reasonable intervals,
and appropriate action should be taken with respect to any difference.
Moreover, direct the Property Officer Designate to properly accomplished the
RPCPPE and furnish the following offices:
a. Original copy - Accounting Unit
b. Duplicate copy - COA Auditor
c. Triplicate copy - Supply and Property Unit
d. Quadruplicate copy - Inventory Committee file

In view of the above observations, we recommended the following courses of
actions for immediate compliance:
a. For delayed Submission of DV, FS, Status of Unliquidated Cash advances,
Report on the Information and Publicity on Programs/Projects
 Promptly submit to the Audit Team the FS, reports, disbursement vouchers
and their supporting documents, etc. to facilitate the complete review and
analysis of accounts to affirm the reliability of the management’s
assertions on the existence/occurrence, validity, accuracy and
completeness of the recorded transactions.
b. For Non-submission of submit RPCPPE
 Conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of PPE and reconcile the
results thereof with the accounting records;
 Maintain the PPE Ledger Card for all group/class of PPE;
 Require the Supply Officer or Designated Official to complete and submit
the RPCPPE with all information as to date of acquisition, cost of assets,
etc., to the Accountant so that the latter can compute and recognize
depreciation charges for all PPE accounts;
 Quarterly Status of Unliquidated Cash Advances prescribed under COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997
c. For Non-submission of GAD Accomplishment Report
 Identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and
assessment as basis of formulating PPAs in GAD Plan and Budget;
 Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep tracl progress
made for consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused
PPAs; and
 Review audit findings and recommendations to determine the remaining
gender issues not addressed by the GAD Plan and Budget.
d. For Non-submission of Report and Utilization of DRRMF
 Require the MDRRMO to submit the aforesaid report every 15th day of
each month
e. For Non-submission of Monthly Bank Reconciliations on all bank accounts
 Verify the abnormal cash balances, reconcile the discrepancies of the
book and bank balances and effect the correcting/adjusting entries for the
discrepancies/errors noted.
 Request monthly bank statements and prepare Monthly BRS on all bank
accounts. In cases of delayed receipt of bank statements, make
representations with the concerned depository banks for prompt
submission of bank statements
f. For Non-submission of copies of contracts and purchase orders with
supporting documents
 Direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and Secretary to furnish
the Audit Team with copies of the aforesaid documents within five (5) days
after perfection.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the timely preparation and
submission of BRS together with the Bank Statements, credit/debit memos
and paid checks for five (5) depository accounts within the prescribed period
provided in COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the journal entry
voucher/s duly supported with documents showing the adjustments noted
during the audit.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand explanation for non-liquidation
of cash advances despite demand letters sent to various accountable
officers.
b. Allow 30 day grace period and when no liquidation or explanation is received
or the explanation is not satisfactory, cause or order the withholding of the
payment of any money due the accountable officer.
c. Execute penal sanction under Section 89 of PD No. 1445 as implemented
under COA Circular No. 90-331 and restated under COA Circular No. 97-002
to compel all accountable officers to promptly render the accounting of the
funds which they have received by reason of their office.
d. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Reports of Liquidation/
Disbursements and journal entry voucher/s duly supported with documents
showing the refunds/adjustments/liquidation.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend that Management:
a) Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, wages, allowances, etc. by
opening of individual ATM savings accounts through a Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with any of the government servicing bank stationed in
Roxas City; and
b) Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the MOA and make
representations for possible establishment of the applicable type of ATM to
the premises of the LGU.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular
97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances
and cause the withholding of salaries of accountable officers who fail to effect
prompt liquidation in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Observe the limitations on the types of PPAs that may be funded under the
20% DF to adhere the requirements laid down under DILG-DBM Joint
Circular DILG-DBM No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, amending DILG-DBM
Joint Circular No. 1 dated September 20, 2005 and Memorandum Circular
No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010; and DILG;
b. Optimally utilize the 20% DF for development projects that shall contribute to
the attainment of desirable economic development and environmental
management outcome and shall partake the nature of investment or capital
expenditure;
c. Discontinue the charging of expenditures enumerated in Section 4.0 of Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-2 of DILG-DBM in the 20% DF; and
d. Ensure that items planned as priorities for 20% DF have not been included in
the PPAs of other funds.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
 On the condition of MRF/dumpsite:
a. Install a MRF and prescribed strict implementation of garbage sorting,
segregation, composting and recycling before final disposal at the
sanitary landfill pursuant to RA 9003;
 On utilization of SWM Fund:
b. Identify PPAs that fall within the permissible projects under SWM where
SWM fund may be allocated and utilized, provided that these activities
must be directly linked to development planning aimed to eliminate and/or
limit the rootcauses that expose and make the community vulnerable to
diseases;
 On waste segregation:
c. Direct the SWM Task Force to intensify information drive/campaign to
concerned residents shall ensure that solid wastes are properly
segregated prior to collection considering the different identification of
solid waste as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" and "special
waste" or “hazardous waste”;
 On record keeping:
d. Maintain the basic records on the operation of SWM required under
Section 42 of RA 9003 and keep track of the current condition of the
dumping site to promote proper monitoring of the program;
 Other consideration:
e. Encourage private organizations and non-government organizations to
join the LGU in its effort of keeping its environment clean, healthy and
green.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the BAC secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with perfected contracts
awarded for the period June 2015 to July 2016 to evaluate whether the claim
of honorarium is valid and within the limits prescribed under DBM Budget
Circular 2004-5 and 5A;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the complete attachments of
successfully completed procurement projects or contract prior to payment of
honoraria to chair and members of the BAC and TWG, and overtime to
government personnel involved in the procurement process and submit the
same within the five (5) working days from the execution of a contract as
prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009; and
c. Require the BAC Secretariat to maintain a log book containing all relevant
information on the procurement process up to the award of contract in a
systematic way so as to provide bases for granting honorarium and overtime
to personnel involve in procurement activities.

Thus, in order to establish a complete inventory of all real properties within LGU
jurisdiction, we recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Assessor to establish proper coordination and linkages
with other heads of departments i.e., Treasury and Accounting or with other
Local and National Government Agencies i.e., Province, Register of Deeds
and DAR among others, to obtain/request
assistance/information/data/documents needed to appraise real property to
its current value or to reflect the prevailing market prices of properties for
RPT purposes and submit the AR to the Municipal Treasurer for the
preparation of Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year;
c. Based on the list, direct the Municipal Accountant shall draw a Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to RPT /SET Receivables and crediting to
Deferred RPT Income/Deferred SET Income for proper recording of
Receivables and Deferred Income at the beginning of the year;

In view of the following, we recommended that Management:
a. Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of
gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as
basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget;
b. Identify and include appropriate PPAs that address priority gender issues in
the GPB;
c. Identify specific gender indicators that would help keep track progress made
for purposes of consistency and sustainability of the identified GAD-focused
PPAs;
d. Determine efficiency and effectiveness of interventions in addressing gender
related issues and concerns taking into account the results of PPAs
evaluation in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries; and
e. Review prior year’s audit findings and recommendations viz-a-viz GAD
accomplishments to determine the remaining gender issues not addressed in
the previous GPB, pursuant to Joint Circular 2012-01 of PCW-NEDA-DBM.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require the
necessary approval from local sanggunian prior to the implementation of the
above projects in the total amount of ₱10.314 million in compliance with the
provision of the law and following the ruling provided in the afore-cited
jurisprudence.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Prioritize the implementation of the identified PPAs based on the LDRRM
Plan and monitor PPAs outcomes to ensure that the impact of calamities to
peoples’ lives and damage to properties are minimized, if not, totally
eliminated;
b. Submit justification why the LDRRM focused-PPAs that could best addressed
the purposes of Disaster Preparedness and Mitigation to reduce future
casualties and property damage were not fully implemented; and
c. Consider in the planning phase the construction choices of projects funded
out of LDRRM fund for long-lasting structures so as to enhance its disaster
preparedness and avoid mass casualties and destruction of properties, if
possible, establish linkages for other funding opportunities.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Earmark funding support for “Research Study” with the end view of collecting
evidence to determine the appropriate programs, projects and activities for a
more equitable and efficient allocation of SEF budget;
b. Direct all concerned DepEd officials, teachers and school heads who are
recipient of financial assistance in connection with their travel, conduct of
various sports and school activities to submit appropriate documents with a
view of determining whether the financial assistance was utilized for its
intended purpose/s;
c. Advise the engineering department to implement budget allocation for the
construction/repair of school buildings, classrooms and facilities including
furnishing of equipment and furniture to improve the learning condition and
needs of children at the early part of the school year; and
d. Submit certification from the School Head that the payment of utilities (Water,
Electric and Telephone Expenses) were not included in the SIP as approved
by the DepEd Superintendent for CY 2016.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management direct all officials
and employees concerned to:
a. Prioritize the implementation of projects under fund transfers and closely
monitor the liquidation of funds by submitting the Liquidation Report (LR)
within the prescribed period,
b. Review the propriety of dormant account and revert all dormant accounts,
unnecessary special, and trust funds to the general fund under DF-DBM-COA
Joint Circular No. 4-2012 dated September 11, 2012,
c. Refund any unused balances of fund recorded under “Due to NGAs and Due
to LGUs” by issuance of check in the name of the concerned agencies,
d. Comply with the 10 day period after the end of each month/end of the agreed
period for the Project, the submission of Report of Checks Issued (RCI) to
report the utilization of the funds, and
e. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization
and liquidation of fund transferred as provided Section 6.7 of COA Circular
No. 94-013 dated December 13,1994 to ensure that the transfer is properly
taken up in the books and used only for the intended purpose and that proper
accounting and reporting are made on the utilization of funds.

Conduct the immediate count of property, plant and equipment and submit the RPCPPE using the format shown in Appendix 49 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU’s, Volume II; and
Reconcile the balances shown in the RPCPPE with the balances per accounting records and prepare the necessary adjustments, if any, for the difference.
